symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral, symmetrical, cold-induced triphasic fingertip colour change (white to blue to red)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The triphasic cold-induced colour change is the defining clinical feature of Raynaud's.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute arterial embolism / thromboembolic digital ischaemia
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bilateral symmetry and colour recovery with warmth are atypical for a single embolic arterial occlusion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Prolonged attack (~45 minutes) only partially resolving despite returning indoors

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Duration and severity beyond the usual pattern raise concern for ischaemia progressing beyond reversible vasospasm, especially in secondary Raynaud's.
Secondary Raynaud's attacks are typically more severe and prolonged than primary.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe deep throbbing ischaemic digital pain with inability to use hands

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ischaemic pain reflects the severity of the vasospastic attack.

symptoms.symptom_name: Established history of milder cold-induced fingertip blanching (known Raynaud's)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A pre-existing Raynaud's pattern makes a severe Raynaud's attack the most likely explanation for tonight's episode.
Oesophageal dysmotility/reflux is the 'E' of CREST and a common systemic-sclerosis manifestation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sclerodactyly — tight, shiny skin over the fingers

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sclerodactyly is a cardinal cutaneous feature of systemic sclerosis and points to SECONDARY Raynaud's.
Buerger's disease is strongly tobacco-associated; a lifelong non-smoker makes it very unlikely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Strong bounding radial and ulnar pulses with intact palmar arches (Allen's test)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute arterial embolism / thromboembolic digital ischaemia
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Intact strong pulses and patent palmar arches indicate preserved macrovascular supply, arguing strongly against a proximal arterial occlusion. Clinician-measured — sealed; the AI Doctor cannot obtain this and must not assume it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Delayed capillary refill (>4 s) that strictly recovers

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Delayed but recovering capillary refill indicates severe but REVERSIBLE microvascular vasospasm rather than fixed occlusion. Clinician-measured — sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: No digital ulcers, gangrene or black discolouration currently

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of tissue loss or fixed colour change is reassuring against established critical ischaemia — but does not exclude evolving ischaemia over the coming hours.
Anti-centromere antibody is highly specific for limited cutaneous systemic sclerosis (CREST). Prior laboratory result — sealed; not confirmable via telehealth in real time.

symptoms.symptom_name: No chest pain or shortness of breath

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No current cardiorespiratory symptoms is mildly reassuring against pulmonary hypertension / interstitial lung disease complications, which still warrant outpatient screening.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: CREST / limited cutaneous systemic sclerosis constellation
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Raynaud's + reflux + sclerodactyly + anti-centromere antibody together identify SECONDARY Raynaud's due to limited systemic sclerosis, changing risk and mandating referral.

Bilateral symmetry, intact pulses, recovering capillary refill and no tissue loss together indicate severe but reversible vasospasm rather than macrovascular arterial occlusion — but three of these four are clinician-measured and unavailable via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person vascular assessment of the hands (pulses, Allen's test, capillary refill, skin/digit inspection)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The examination the AI Doctor cannot perform; needed tonight to exclude critical/fixed ischaemia and arterial occlusion.
Confirms the underlying connective-tissue disease driving secondary Raynaud's; arranged by GP/rheumatology, not emergently.
Distinguishes primary from secondary Raynaud's and supports a systemic-sclerosis diagnosis; specialist outpatient test.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent CT/catheter arteriography or thrombophilia/embolic work-up
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Over-investigation for a bilateral, symmetrical, cold-induced attack that is reperfusing with intact pulses; pursuing an embolic work-up here would be an error of commission unless the in-person exam reveals a fixed, asymmetric, pulseless ischaemic digit.
